Nothing new can come from infrastructure. Everything that is needed is already there. Anything "new" is useless if in practice no apps need it. 

New things will only start happening once regular companies start using crypto to enhance their services to clients. This includes the public administration btw.

With the shift in tone in favor of crypto, this might happen in the near future, but the industry needs to be ready to respond with real proven and working solutions. 

This last part boils down to having a real solution for key management and privacy, and focusing on ultra simple solutions with exceptional value propositions.

text:
Some additional points:

1) This is undoubtedly an error. If you define "the algorithm" goal as "estimating the relevance of a post for a given user", it's obvious that both posts should have similar "relevance" values, but they get totally different measurements (estimations).

2) You could argue that the algorithm worked well, but people behave "erroneously" by overreacting to the conspiracy-worded version of the post. I think "the algorithm" should compensate for these obvious biases.

3) This type of A/B tests could be used to calibrate the algorithm. A good algorithm would give the same "reach" to both posts, independent of the wording.
